items.q: What is the enhanced greenhouse effect?
items.correct: The strengthening of the natural greenhouse effect by human activities (mainly burning fossil fuels) which add CO2 and other gases to the atmosphere.
items.misconception: The greenhouse effect is entirely man-made and harmful.
items.random_wrong: It is the hole in the ozone layer letting in more sun.
items.why: The natural greenhouse effect is essential for life — without it Earth would be -18 degrees C. The problem is enhancement. Students also confuse it with the (separate) ozone hole.

items.q: What causes global sea level rise?
items.correct: Two main factors: thermal expansion of warming seawater, and the melting of land-based ice (glaciers and ice sheets) adding water to oceans.
items.misconception: Sea level rises mainly because Arctic sea ice is melting.
items.random_wrong: Sea level rises because of underwater volcanoes pushing it up.
items.why: Floating sea ice already displaces its weight in water — its melting does not raise sea level. The contributors are LAND ice (Greenland, Antarctica) and thermal expansion.

items.q: What is the difference between primary and secondary effects of an earthquake?
items.correct: Primary effects occur immediately as a direct result of shaking (building collapse, deaths); secondary effects follow afterwards (fires, tsunamis, disease, homelessness).
items.misconception: Primary effects are the worst effects and secondary effects are minor.
items.random_wrong: Primary effects happen first and secondary effects happen the next day.
items.why: Secondary effects can be far worse — the 2011 Tohoku tsunami killed more than the shaking itself. The distinction is about cause (direct vs cascading), not severity or strict timing.

items.q: Which area is part of the UK's wealthier 'South'?
items.why: The answer examiners want is: South-East England. “Newcastle” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.

items.q: Which area is famous as a UK science park hub?
items.why: It's (Silicon Fen). “Blackpool” is the classic near-miss here; knowing why it's wrong is worth as much as knowing the right answer.

items.q: In which year did the UK officially leave the EU (Brexit)?
items.why: Exact answer: 2020. Estimation gets you near it; only the anchored number survives the exam hall.
